quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! Their wings rotate in a full circle, allowing them to hover in place and even fly upside down for short distances. These tiny acrobats beat their wings up to 80 times per second.

short_read.title: The Secret Language of Trees
short_read.teaser: Scientists have discovered that trees communicate with each other through an underground network of fungi. They share nutrients, send warnings about insects, and even help their neighbors in times of need.

medium_read.title: The Return of Sea Otters to the Pacific Northwest
medium_read.teaser: Once hunted nearly to extinction, sea otters are making a remarkable comeback along the Pacific Coast. Their return is transforming kelp forest ecosystems and bringing joy to wildlife watchers throughout the region.

long_read.title: The Thousand-Year-Old Hungarian Crown
long_read.teaser: The Holy Crown of St. Stephen has survived wars, revolutions, and even a stay at Fort Knox. This extraordinary journey of Hungary's most sacred symbol reveals centuries of European history and the enduring spirit of the Hungarian people.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer Fawning Season
port_angeles.description: June is fawning season for the black-tailed deer throughout the Olympic Peninsula. You might spot does with their spotted fawns in the early morning or evening around Park View Villas. Remember, if you see a fawn alone, the mother is likely nearby feeding and will return.

puzzle_1.question: A ruby-throated hummingbird's heart beats about ___ times per minute.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Yes! That's right — 1,200 beats per minute! These tiny birds have incredibly fast hearts to fuel their amazing flying abilities.
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute — that's 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: The answer is 1,200 beats per minute! That's about 20 times faster than a human heart. These tiny birds need all that energy for their incredible flying.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To put this in perspective, a hummingbird's heart rate can reach 1,260 beats per minute during flight, but slows to about 50 beats per minute when they enter torpor (a hibernation-like state) at night to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is butterfly in Hungarian — such a beautiful, flowing word for such a delicate creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The answer is pillangó! It's such a lovely word. (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méhecske means little bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: The Hungarian word 'pillangó' is thought to come from the word 'pille' meaning a light, fluttering thing. Hungary is home to over 200 species of butterflies, including the beautiful Swallowtail.

puzzle_3.question: What is the tallest tree species found in Olympic National Park?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Excellent! Sitka Spruce trees can grow over 300 feet tall in the Olympic rainforests — they're truly majestic giants of the Pacific Northwest!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's the Sitka Spruce! These magnificent trees can reach over 300 feet tall in the Olympic Peninsula's rainforests, making them some of the tallest trees in the world.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Sitka Spruce thrives in the misty, temperate rainforests of Olympic National Park. Some specimens are over 500 years old, and their enormous trunks can be 16 feet in diameter.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
